raw_title: Resistant and Misinformed: Understanding the Role of Elite Perceptions and Resistance Strategies Against COVID-19 Reporting in Legacy Media in Adopting COVID-19 Misperceptions
title_en: Resistant and Misinformed: Understanding the Role of Elite Perceptions and Resistance Strategies Against COVID-19 Reporting in Legacy Media in Adopting COVID-19 Misperceptions
title_zh: 抵抗与误信：理解精英认知和抵抗策略在传统媒体COVID-19报道中对COVID-19错误认知采纳的作用
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了为什么有些人会相信关于COVID-19的错误信息。它特别关注精英人群（如政治人物、专家）如何看待传统媒体的疫情报道，以及他们如何抵抗这些报道，最终导致人们采纳错误认知。研究想了解精英的态度和抵抗策略如何影响公众对疫情信息的信任。
plain_summary_en: This study explores why some people believe false information about COVID-19. It focuses specifically on how elites (like politicians and experts) perceive traditional media's pandemic reporting and how they resist it, leading to the adoption of misperceptions. The research aims to understand how elite attitudes and resistance strategies influence public trust in pandemic information.
research_direction_zh: 该研究属于传播学和新闻学领域，关注媒体信任、错误信息传播和精英影响。它探讨了在公共卫生危机中，传统媒体的报道如何被精英群体抵抗，进而影响公众对科学信息的接受。研究方向包括媒体效果、政治传播和健康传播的交叉点。
research_direction_en: This research falls within communication studies and journalism, focusing on media trust, misinformation dissemination, and elite influence. It examines how traditional media reporting during public health crises is resisted by elite groups, affecting public acceptance of scientific information. The research direction includes intersections of media effects, political communication, and health communication.
